fact: February 24 is exactly 55 days into the year - that's like counting to 55 Mississippi's every single day since New Year's!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when maple trees start getting ready to make syrup by pumping sugar water up their trunks like tiny tree blood!
February 24 falls smack in the middle of Pisces season - the fish zodiac sign that's supposed to make people extra dreamy and weird.
In Antarctica right now, penguins are dealing with 18 hours of daylight because it's late summer down there - imagine trying to sleep with the sun blazing!
This date sits exactly 310 days away from New Year's Eve - you could literally count down 'only 310 more sleeps until next year!'
Earthworms in cold places are starting to wiggle closer to the surface because the ground is slowly warming up, even though it still feels freezing to us!
February 24 happens during the time when the sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day in most places - that's 14 extra minutes of daylight just this week!
In Japan, plum blossoms are bursting open right now, covering entire hillsides in pink and white flowers while snow might still be on the ground!
This is prime time for seeing the constellation Orion perfectly positioned in the evening sky - he's like a giant space warrior standing guard over winter!
February 24 marks when many hibernating animals are at their skinniest because they've been burning fat for months without eating - like the world's longest diet!
In the Southern Hemisphere, this date falls during harvest season when farmers are collecting corn, grapes, and other crops under blazing hot sun!
